posts.text: Middle East wars are now scheduled from 9pm Friday to 10.30pm Sunday (GMT). That’s it. Fighting outside designed non-market hours isn’t allowed.
posts.summary: Javier Blas is satirizing how Middle East conflicts appear timed to avoid disrupting oil markets during weekday trading hours, suggesting geopolitical tensions are now constrained to non-market periods. The comment implies oil market stability has become a de facto boundary condition for regional conflict timing.

posts.text: Barak's Ravid's tweet was right on time (as usual) - but tanker transits through Hormuz are back to crisis levels. https://t.co/fqDUtHmZUo
posts.summary: Tanker transits through the Strait of Hormuz have returned to crisis levels, suggesting potential disruptions to oil supply flows through one of the world's most critical energy chokepoints.

posts.text: You guys. Do yourselves a favor and listen to alternative views. So what, someone doesn't agree with you, listen up to their argument. That will either lead you to solidify your thesis or question it.
posts.summary: This post contains general advice about engaging with alternative viewpoints and has no direct reference to oil markets or their implications.

posts.text: The first of the group of Saudi supertankers (⤵️) has now exited the Strait of Hormuz and is now in the Gulf of Oman (on her way to Japan). The Zaynah VLCC exited Sun-Mon in dark mode. Another laden tanker has gone dark, and two other remain visible at the Saudi oil port. https://t.co/bcAJdDXv6Z
posts.summary: A Saudi supertanker carrying oil has departed for Japan via the Strait of Hormuz, with additional laden tankers also turning off their tracking systems, suggesting increased Saudi crude shipments to Asian markets. The movement of multiple VLCCs from Saudi ports indicates potential changes in crude oil export flows and trading patterns.

posts.text: 7 IN A ROW! The West Coast is seeing the biggest relief at the gas pump as the national average fell for the 7th straight week to $3.78/gal, while diesel fell 11 cents to $4.88/gal... prices should fall again in most states this week, while some could cycle
posts.summary: Gas prices have declined for seven consecutive weeks, reaching $3.78 per gallon nationally, with diesel dropping to $4.88 per gallon, suggesting continued downward pressure on fuel costs in the near term. The trend indicates broader softening in oil market conditions, particularly benefiting the West Coast.

posts.text:
Rystad Energy sees Middle East oil flows fully recovering before the end of the year.

Why? 

1. Most large oil production and export assets were left unscathed. 

2. After months of lost income, Middle East oil producers are now more incentivized than ever to ramp oil production
posts.summary: Middle East oil production is expected to return to normal levels by year-end, driven by intact infrastructure and producers' motivation to recover lost revenue. This recovery could increase global oil supply and potentially ease upward pressure on prices.

posts.text:
US SPR crude inventories fell by ~5.5mb w/w to a multi year low of 325.7mb last week #oott      

Sour down ~2.7mb to 205.5mb    
Sweet down ~2.9mb to 120.9mb https://t.co/gnv7sJziwf
posts.summary: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve crude inventories declined to multi-year lows, with total stocks falling 5.5 million barrels to 325.7 million barrels, while both sour crude (down 2.7mb) and sweet crude (down 2.9mb) inventories also decreased. The continued drawdown reflects ongoing depletion of the nation's emergency oil reserves.

posts.text:
No immediate damage reported after magnitude 4.6 aftershock in Venezuela
CARACAS, June 29 (Reuters) - A 4.6-magnitude aftershock centered at a depth of 10 km (6 miles) hit north of the Venezuelan capital Caracas early on Monday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
posts.summary: A 4.6-magnitude aftershock struck near Caracas, Venezuela early Monday with no immediate damage reported, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. The article provides no information regarding potential impacts on Venezuela's oil production or energy infrastructure.

posts.text: Europe risks starting winter with gas stocks at 15-year low https://t.co/Ov9WjYqnXk
posts.summary: Europe faces potential supply vulnerabilities heading into winter due to critically low natural gas inventories at 15-year lows, which could create energy security pressures and potentially support crude oil demand as an alternative fuel source.

posts.text:
With inventories impossible to track now, traders increasingly rely on curve dynamics as a proxy for forward s&d expectations. This "deceleration of backwardation" signal (curve-momentum) turned decidely bearish and is behind the recent wave of selling.
#oott https://t.co/pYz9eUzoFD
posts.summary: With inventory data becoming unreliable, oil traders are using the futures curve as a signal for supply and demand expectations, and a weakening backwardation pattern has recently turned bearish, driving selling pressure in the market.

posts.text: COT update: Crowded or one-sided positioning raise the risk of sharp countertrend moves https://t.co/mTk1oJHuFq via @saxobank
posts.summary: Ole Hansen notes that extreme or lopsided positioning in Commitment of Traders (COT) data increases the likelihood of sharp reversals, a risk factor relevant to oil market trading positioning. The warning suggests traders should be cautious when positioning becomes too crowded in one direction.

posts.text:
Gulf container links resume

Three Global Feeder Shipping vessels entered the Gulf between 26 and 28 June, becoming the first containerships with commercial links to major carriers to do so since the start of the conflict. GFS Genesis, at 4,350 TEU, sailed from Nhava Sheva and https://t.co/s0c4flx38h
posts.summary: Container shipping services have resumed in the Gulf with the arrival of three Global Feeder Shipping vessels in late June, marking the first commercial containership activity linked to major carriers since the conflict began. The oil market implications of this development are not addressed in the provided text.

posts.text: Engineers urge Venezuela to audit state housing after deadly quake https://t.co/qgvmeD9Ydm
posts.summary: Engineers in Venezuela are calling for an audit of state housing following a deadly earthquake, which could potentially impact infrastructure maintenance and economic resources in the country. The headline suggests structural vulnerabilities in Venezuela's built environment, though the text provided does not contain specific information about direct oil market implications.

posts.text:
As the effects of the energy crisis ripple across the globe, strengthening efficiency progress can offer key benefits for energy security & affordability.

Decision-makers will discuss this opportunity at next week's Global Energy Efficiency Conference 👉 https://t.co/GinMIFak0z https://t.co/netnHbuvOB
posts.summary: The IEA highlights that improving energy efficiency can strengthen energy security and affordability amid global energy crisis impacts, with decision-makers set to explore this opportunity at an upcoming conference. While the post focuses on efficiency broadly, stronger efficiency measures could help moderate overall energy demand and potentially ease pressure on crude oil markets.

posts.text: Refineries in northern tier states are not built to run cooling towers at ambient temperatures above 100 degrees F.  This week's heat wave targets millions of barrels per day of production in IL, IN, KY, OH, MI, PA, DE, and NJ.  A critical test of the power grid looms as well.
posts.summary: Extreme heat poses a significant threat to U.S. oil refining capacity, as northern refineries lack cooling infrastructure for temperatures exceeding 100°F, potentially reducing millions of barrels per day of production across eight states. The heat wave also creates concurrent strain on the electrical grid that supports these operations.

posts.text:
India leads incremental long-term oil demand growth

The primary sources of long-term oil demand growth are India, Other Asia, the Middle East, Africa and Latin America. Demand in these regions is set to increase by 25.2 mb/d between 2025 and 2050, with India alone adding 8.1 https://t.co/DBZ0s8SSTV
posts.summary: India is projected to account for nearly one-third of global oil demand growth between 2025 and 2050, adding 8.1 million barrels per day as developing regions in Asia, Africa, and Latin America collectively drive a 25.2 mb/d increase. This concentration of demand growth in emerging economies underscores a significant shift in oil consumption patterns away from developed markets.

posts.text:
WATCH NOW | We're LIVE from @IEA’s 11th Annual Global Conference on Energy Efficiency!

Senior decision-makers are discussing the benefits stronger #EfficiencyAction can bring for energy security & affordability amid the current energy crisis 👇 https://t.co/dhHhd28UXn
posts.summary: The IEA's conference highlights how energy efficiency improvements can strengthen energy security and affordability during the current energy crisis, which has implications for reducing oil demand pressures. Stronger efficiency action could help moderate energy costs and security concerns in the oil market by decreasing overall consumption needs.

posts.text: Last week saw huge exodus of financial length from Brent crude oil - - about 40-million barrels. Long positioning in Brent has been higher some 80% of the time in the last two and five years and about 95% over the longer ten-year period. Scant speculative buying as June ends.
posts.summary: Financial investors exited approximately 40 million barrels of long positions in Brent crude oil last week, marking a significant shift given that long positioning has historically been elevated 80-95% of the time over the past two to ten years. The exodus reflects minimal speculative buying interest as June concludes.

posts.text: GLOBAL JET FUEL - import dependence and vulnerability: https://t.co/xCYNlOdKT2
posts.summary: JKem Energy highlights that global jet fuel markets face import dependence and vulnerability risks. The post suggests potential supply chain exposure in aviation fuel markets without providing additional details in the text itself.

posts.text: The sourcing of crude oil for the 14 refineries in Louisiana has been shifting over the past few years and the pace of that shift is picking up as more U.S. Gulf production flows to Texas (and less flows to the Bayou State), new pipeline projects come online, and refineries https://t.co/L0p3zsyZ26
posts.summary: Louisiana's 14 refineries are increasingly sourcing crude oil from outside the state as U.S. Gulf production shifts toward Texas and new pipeline infrastructure alters traditional supply routes. This supply reallocation reflects evolving logistics and production dynamics in the Gulf region's refining sector.

posts.text:
"As long as the vessels that have been stuck are coming out that is one thing, but in a few weeks time when we need vessels to go back in, that’s when the rubbers going to hit the road."

Energy Aspects Founder and Director of Market Intelligence, @ea_amrita, joined @SquawkCNBC https://t.co/eTEwAJqgjz
posts.summary: Energy Aspects highlights that while the current clearing of stuck vessels provides temporary relief, the real test for oil markets will come in the coming weeks when vessels need to resume normal operations, suggesting potential supply disruptions ahead. The comment implies current vessel blockages may have more significant market consequences once shipping routes need to return to regular patterns.

posts.text:

posts.summary: OPEC has released its World Oil Outlook 2026 report in multiple languages to improve global access to energy market information. The move reflects the international nature of oil markets and OPEC's effort to reach a broader audience with its market analysis and forecasts.

posts.summary: The US power sector is experiencing a record $200 billion M&A boom driven by AI demand, which could shift investment focus toward electricity infrastructure rather than traditional oil and gas assets.

The company's expertise underscores concerns about potential disruptions to jet fuel markets from regional instability.

posts.text: Qatar temporarily halts all marine vessels after citizen killed by shrapnel https://t.co/uiaaAiJz57
posts.summary: Qatar has temporarily halted all marine vessel operations following a citizen's death from shrapnel, which could potentially disrupt oil and gas exports from one of the world's largest liquefied natural gas producers. The impact on global energy markets will depend on the duration of the halt and whether it affects crude oil shipments or LNG operations.
